content: In Romans 8:28, "all things work together for good to those who love God." What does the Greek word for "work together" mean?
explanation: Sunergeo (συνεργέω) means "to work together," all things working together for our good.

content: In Romans 12:2, "do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewal of your mind." What does the Greek word for "transformed" mean?
explanation: Metamorphoo (μεταμορφόω) means "to transform from within," like a butterfly transforming from a caterpillar.

content: Romans 3:23 says who has sinned?
explanation: Romans 3:23 - For all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God. No exceptions.

content: According to Romans 5:8, when did God show His love for us?
explanation: Romans 5:8 - While we were still sinners, Christ died for us. Unconditional love.

content: Romans 6:23 says: "The wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is ___"
explanation: Romans 6:23 - Sin leads to death, but God's free gift is eternal life through Jesus.

content: According to Romans 8:38-39, what can separate us from the love of God?
explanation: Romans 8:38-39 - Nothing can separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus.
options: Nothing - neither death, nor life, nor anything

content: Romans 10:9 names what two conditions for salvation?
explanation: Romans 10:9 - If you confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord and believe in your heart that God raised Him from the dead, you will be saved.
options: Confessing Jesus as Lord with the mouth and believing He rose

content: Romans 12:2 urges us not to conform to this world but to be renewed how?
explanation: Romans 12:2 - Transformation begins with the mind. When the mind is renewed by God's Word, behavior changes.

content: Paul declared: "I am not ashamed of the gospel, for it is the ___ of God for the salvation of everyone who believes"
explanation: Romans 1:16 - The gospel is the power (dunamis) of God for salvation.

content: Having been justified by faith, we have ___ with God
explanation: Romans 5:1 - Justification by faith brings peace with God. Sin is wiped away.

content: Romans 5:3-4: Suffering produces endurance, endurance produces ___, and that produces hope
explanation: Hardship is the process that builds character and steadfast hope.

content: Paul asks: Should we go on sinning so that grace may abound?
explanation: Romans 6:2 - Grace is no excuse to sin. The true believer has died to sin.
options: By no means! We have died to sin

content: Romans 8:31: "If God is for us, who can be ___ us?"
explanation: If God stands with us, no one can successfully oppose us.

content: Paul exhorts believers to present their bodies as what?
explanation: Romans 12:1 - True worship is offering one's whole life to God, not just at church.
options: A living and holy sacrifice, pleasing to God (spiritual worship)

content: What does Paul say he is not ashamed of?
explanation: Romans 1:16: The gospel is the power of God for salvation to everyone who believes.

content: According to Romans 3:23, what is true of all people?
explanation: Romans 3:23: All have sinned and fall short of the glory of God.
options: All have sinned and fall short of the glory of God

content: By what are we justified?
explanation: Romans 5:1: Having been justified by faith, we have peace with God.

content: What are the wages of sin?
explanation: Romans 6:23: The wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life.

content: What is true of those who are in Christ Jesus?
explanation: Romans 8:1: There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.

content: Paul calls believers to present their bodies as what?
explanation: Romans 12:1: Present your bodies as a living and holy sacrifice, pleasing to God.

content: Why is the wrath of God revealed from heaven against all ungodliness?
explanation: Romans 1:18: Because they suppress the truth by their unrighteousness.
options: They are unfaithful to their countrymen

content: How was Abraham justified according to Romans 4?
explanation: Romans 4:3-5: Abraham believed God, and it was counted to him as righteousness.

content: Adam and Christ are compared to show what?
explanation: Romans 5:18-19: Through the obedience of one man (Christ), many are made righteous.
options: The Law is better than grace
Sin came through one man; righteousness through one man

content: What inner conflict does Romans 7 describe?
explanation: Romans 7:22-25: I delight in the law of God in my inner being, but I see another law in my members.
options: The flesh vs. the law of the mind

content: How does the Spirit help our weakness in prayer?
explanation: Romans 8:26: The Spirit intercedes for us with groanings too deep for words.
options: By interceding with groanings too deep for words

content: What does God's election (Jacob/Esau) emphasize?
explanation: Romans 9:11-16: Not because of works, but because of Him who calls — to display God's purpose.

content: What is the condition for salvation according to Romans 10:9-10?
explanation: Romans 10:9-10: If you confess with your mouth and believe in your heart, you will be saved.
options: Confess with the mouth, believe with the heart

content: What does the image of grafting in the olive tree teach?
explanation: Romans 11:17-24: Wild branches are grafted into the cultivated root — Gentiles share by faith.
options: The Jewish people are cut off
A new tree replaces the old

content: How does Romans 13 teach about our relationship with governing authorities?
explanation: Romans 13:1-7: Authority comes from God; pay taxes and show honor.
options: Submit, for they are God's servants

content: What does the "golden chain" of salvation (foreknew, predestined...) teach?
explanation: Romans 8:29-30: Those whom He foreknew He also predestined... called, justified, and glorified.
options: Grace is for one nation only

content: What do the "vessels of wrath" and "vessels of mercy" highlight?
explanation: Romans 9:22-24: God displays His glory upon vessels of mercy, which He prepared beforehand for glory.
options: The strength of the Jewish people

content: What does the doxology at the end of chapter 11 highlight about God?
explanation: Romans 11:33-36: From Him and through Him and to Him are all things; to Him be glory forever.
options: His infinite wisdom, judgment, and glory

content: What does Paul teach about the one weak in faith?
explanation: Romans 14:1-4: Do not pass judgment on the weak in faith; each stands or falls before the Lord.
options: Do not pass judgment; God can make him stand

content: What is Paul's missionary motto in Romans 15?
explanation: Romans 15:20-21: Not to build on another's foundation, but to go where Christ has not been named.
options: Preach the gospel where no one has preached it

content: How does Paul address the Roman believers in Romans 1:7?
explanation: Romans 1:7 — 'To all those in Rome who are loved by God and called to be saints.'
options: Loved by God and called to be saints

content: According to Romans 1:20, through what are God's invisible attributes perceived?
explanation: Romans 1:20 — 'For his invisible attributes, namely, his eternal power and divine nature, have been clearly perceived, ever since the creation of the world, in the things that have been made.'

content: What does Romans 2:1 warn about those who judge others?
explanation: Romans 2:1 — 'Therefore you have no excuse, O man, every one of you who judges. For in passing judgment on another you condemn yourself, because you, the judge, practice the very same things.'
options: Condemn themselves; for they do the same things

content: What does Romans 2:11 say about God in relation to people?
explanation: Romans 2:11 — 'For God shows no partiality.'

content: Romans 3:10-12 quotes Scripture about humanity: 'None ___'
explanation: Romans 3:10-12 — 'None is righteous, no, not one... no one does good, not even one.'

content: According to Romans 4:16, by what is the promise to Abraham's offspring given?
explanation: Romans 4:16 — 'That is why it depends on faith, in order that the promise may rest on grace and be guaranteed to all his offspring.'

content: Through whom does Romans 5:5 say God's love is poured out into our hearts?
explanation: Romans 5:5 — 'God's love has been poured into our hearts through the Holy Spirit who has been given to us.'
options: Through the Holy Spirit who has been given

content: What does Romans 6:4 say baptism symbolizes?
explanation: Romans 6:4 — 'We were buried therefore with him by baptism into death, in order that, just as Christ was raised from the dead by the glory of the Father, we too might walk in newness of life.'
options: Buried with Christ in death and walking in newness of life

content: Romans 7:7 asks: 'Is the law sin?' — How does Paul answer?
explanation: Romans 7:7 — 'By no means! Yet if it had not been for the law, I would not have known sin. For I would not have known what it is to covet if the law had not said, You shall not covet.'
options: By no means! Through the law I came to know sin

content: According to Romans 8:14-17, those led by the Spirit are what?
explanation: Romans 8:14 — 'For all who are led by the Spirit of God are sons of God.'
options: Sons of God, fellow heirs with Christ

content: Romans 8:18 compares present sufferings with what?
explanation: Romans 8:18 — 'For I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worth comparing with the glory that is to be revealed to us.'
options: The glory that is to be revealed (not worth comparing)

content: What deep love for Israel does Paul express in Romans 9:3?
explanation: Romans 9:3 — 'For I could wish that I myself were accursed and cut off from Christ for the sake of my brothers, my kinsmen according to the flesh.'
options: Wishing he himself were accursed and cut off from Christ for their sake

content: Romans 10:17 says faith comes through what?
explanation: Romans 10:17 — 'So faith comes from hearing, and hearing through the word of Christ.'
options: Through hearing — and hearing through the word of Christ

content: Romans 11:6 says if salvation is by grace, then it is no longer by what?
explanation: Romans 11:6 — 'But if it is by grace, it is no longer on the basis of works; otherwise grace would no longer be grace.'

content: How does Romans 12:9-10 say brotherly love should be?
explanation: Romans 12:9-10 — 'Let love be genuine. Abhor what is evil; hold fast to what is good. Love one another with brotherly affection. Outdo one another in showing honor.'
options: Genuine, abhorring evil, holding to good, with brotherly affection, outdoing in honor

content: What does Romans 12:19 teach about an enemy?
explanation: Romans 12:19 — 'Beloved, never avenge yourselves, but leave it to the wrath of God, for it is written, Vengeance is mine, I will repay, says the Lord.'
options: Never avenge yourselves; leave it to the wrath of God

content: What does Romans 12:21 teach must overcome evil?
explanation: Romans 12:21 — 'Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.'

content: What is the only debt Romans 13:8 says remains?
explanation: Romans 13:8 — 'Owe no one anything, except to love each other.'
options: The debt to love one another

content: What does Romans 14:13 urge believers not to do regarding food matters?
explanation: Romans 14:13 — 'Therefore let us not pass judgment on one another any longer, but rather decide never to put a stumbling block or hindrance in the way of a brother.'
options: Not pass judgment on each other or place a stumbling block

content: Romans 15:1-2 — what should the strong do for the weak?
explanation: Romans 15:1 — 'We who are strong have an obligation to bear with the failings of the weak, and not to please ourselves.'
options: Bear with their failings and please them for their building up

content: Romans 15:13 prays God of hope to fill believers with what 'abounding by the power of the Holy Spirit'?
explanation: Romans 15:13 — 'May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that by the power of the Holy Spirit you may abound in hope.'

content: Whom does Paul commend in Romans 16:1-2 as 'a servant of the church at Cenchreae'?
explanation: Romans 16:1 — 'I commend to you our sister Phoebe, a servant of the church at Cenchreae.'

content: Whom does Romans 16:17 warn about in the church?
explanation: Romans 16:17 — 'I appeal to you, brothers, to watch out for those who cause divisions and create obstacles contrary to the doctrine that you have been taught; avoid them.'
options: Those who cause divisions and teach contrary to apostolic doctrine

content: What does Romans 8:9 say about anyone who does not have the Spirit of Christ?
explanation: Romans 8:9 — 'Anyone who does not have the Spirit of Christ does not belong to him.'

content: What does Romans 4:25 say Jesus was delivered up for, and raised for?
explanation: Romans 4:25 — 'Who was delivered up for our trespasses and raised for our justification.'
options: For the law; for the patriarchs
For our trespasses; for our justification

content: Whom does Romans 13:14 urge believers to put on, and what to make no provision for?
explanation: Romans 13:14 — 'Put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, to gratify its desires.'
options: Put on the law; not on possessions
Put on the priesthood; not many words
Put on the Lord Jesus Christ; make no provision for the flesh
